column_desc: Unique model code that identifies a specific vehicle model and variant combination in the master data. This alphanumeric code serves as the primary key for the vehicle model dimension and is used to link vehicle specifications to repair orders, sales transactions, and inventory records.

joins.description: Use this join to enrich repair orders with detailed vehicle model specifications including variant, fuel type, transmission, and seating capacity for model-specific service analysis.

column_desc: Descriptive name of the vehicle model that combines model family, variant, drivetrain, seating capacity, and key features. Provides a human-readable identification of the vehicle for use in reports, customer communications, and operational systems.
examples: THAR LX D MT 4WD 4S HT BB
THAR AX OPT D MT 4WD 4S CT EVR WHT
XUV700 AX7 PET AT 7 SEATER BLU
XUV700 AX7 L DSL AT 7 SEATER DPFST DT

column_desc: Model group code that groups related vehicle models under a common family or platform. Used for aggregating similar models for reporting, analysis, and marketing purposes. Examples include THAR for Thar models, X700 for XUV700 models, X300 for XUV300 models.

Variant code that identifies a specific configuration within a model family. Differentiates between various trim levels, feature sets, and options available for the same base model. Used for inventory management and specification tracking.

Number of seats available in the vehicle configuration. Critical for vehicle classification, customer requirements, and regulatory compliance. Used in customer queries, inventory filtering, and vehicle categorization.

Model description as it appears on sales invoices and official documents. May be formatted differently from the standard model description to meet invoicing requirements or commercial naming conventions.
examples: THAR LX D MT 4WD 4S HT BB
THAR AX OPT D MT 4WD 4S CT EVR WHT
XUV700 AX7 PET AT 7 SEATER BLU
XUV700 AX7 L DSL AT 7 SEATER DPFST DT

column_desc: Family code that groups models under a broader vehicle family classification. Used for strategic analysis, portfolio management, and understanding market positioning of different vehicle families.

Color code that identifies the standard exterior color of the vehicle. Used in vehicle configuration, inventory management, and customer specifications. Often combines a color code prefix with a descriptive color name.

Product division code indicating which product line or brand the vehicle belongs to. Examples include TH (Thar), IX (XUV), BS (Bolero), IW (Scorpio). Used for product-level reporting and analysis across the organization.

joins.description: Use this join to connect vehicle models with dealers authorized to sell and service specific product divisions. Helps analyze dealer network coverage for different vehicle brands.

column_desc: Batch or manufacturing batch identifier for the vehicle model. May indicate production batches, special editions, or manufacturing variations. Used for quality tracking and recall management.
Original Equipment Manufacturer (OEM) model code used in manufacturing and supply chain systems. May differ from the sales model code and is used for integration with production and parts systems.
Sales model group used for organizing models in sales reporting and analysis. Helps group similar models for sales performance tracking and market analysis.
Numeric value used for ordering model groups in reports and dropdowns. Ensures consistent presentation order of models in user interfaces and printed materials.
Market segment code that classifies the vehicle according to its target market, such as PERSONAL (private use), COMMERCIAL (business use), or other segments. Used for market analysis and targeting.

joins.description: Use this join to analyze service patterns and repair costs across different market segments, comparing personal vehicle service requirements with commercial vehicle needs.

column_desc: Detailed description of the vehicle variant, including trim level, drivetrain, fuel type, and transmission. Provides comprehensive specification information for accurate vehicle identification.
examples: AX OPT D MT 4WD CT BS6.2

column_desc: Descriptive name of the vehicle family. Provides a human-readable name for the family code, such as 'THAR CRDe NGT530R' or 'New THAR Diesel MT'. Used in reporting and customer-facing communications.

Active indicator showing whether the model is currently active in the system. 'Y' indicates the model is available for sales and service, 'N' indicates discontinued or inactive models. Used for filtering active models in operational systems.
Descriptive name of the model group. Provides a human-readable name for the model group code, such as 'THAR' or 'XUV700'. Used for grouping and reporting at the model family level.

Type of fuel the vehicle uses for propulsion. Common values include DIESEL, PETROL, ELECTRIC, HYBRID, or CNG. Critical for service requirements, parts inventory, and environmental compliance tracking.
Emission standard that the vehicle complies with, such as BS4, BS6, BS6.2 (Bharat Stage emission standards). Used for regulatory compliance, environmental impact assessment, and service requirements.

Drivetrain configuration of the vehicle, indicating which wheels receive power. Common values include 2WD (Two-Wheel Drive), 4WD (Four-Wheel Drive), AWD (All-Wheel Drive), or FWD (Front-Wheel Drive). Affects vehicle capability and service requirements.

Trim level description that specifies the feature set and equipment level of the vehicle variant. Includes information about interior, exterior, and technology features. Used for precise vehicle specification and feature-based analysis.
examples: AX OPT D MT 4WD CT BS6.2

column_desc: Type of transmission system in the vehicle. Indicates whether the vehicle has MANUAL, AUTOMATIC (AT), or other transmission types. Critical for service requirements, parts compatibility, and customer preferences.

Repair Order number that uniquely identifies the service transaction in the system. This alphanumeric identifier links customer verbatim comments to the corresponding repair order and is used for cross-referencing with other service-related tables.

joins.description: Use this join to connect customer-reported issues with the complete repair order header information, including vehicle details, service dates, and billing amounts for comprehensive service analysis.

column_desc: Surrogate key uniquely identifying the repair order header record in the data warehouse. This system-generated identifier serves as the primary key for the customer verbatim table and enables efficient joining with other fact tables without relying on business keys.

joins.description: Mandatory join to associate customer verbatim comments with the corresponding repair order header. Essential for analyzing customer-reported issues in the context of the complete service transaction.
Use this join to analyze the relationship between customer-reported issues and the actual labor performed. Helps identify if reported problems align with the services rendered.
Use this join to connect customer-reported issues with parts replaced during service. Enables analysis of common part replacements for specific customer complaints.

column_desc: Free-text description of the customer's service request or complaint as reported verbatim by the customer. Captures the exact language used by customers to describe issues, including colloquial terms, specific concerns, and service requests. Critical for understanding customer perspective and identifying common pain points.
examples: PAID SERVICE @1,000 KM AS PER SERVICE SCHEDULE
AC PROPER COOLING NHI KAR RAHI HAI
CLU-Clutch Chatters/Grabs/Slips/Jerks/Judder-CLUTCH JUDDRING WHEN VEHICLE RELEASE CONT LAST 7 DAYS

column_desc: Three-letter code representing the high-level category or system group of the customer's reported issue. Examples include ENG (Engine), TRN (Transmission), CLU (Clutch), HVA (HVAC/Climate Control), BOD (Body), CLN (Cooling), GEN (General). Used for categorizing and aggregating customer complaints by major vehicle systems.

joins.description: Use this self-reference to map verbatim group codes to their descriptive names for reporting and analysis when the description is needed alongside the code.

column_desc: Standardized code that classifies the customer's reported issue according to a predefined service issue taxonomy. Combines the verbatim group (e.g., ENG) with a numeric identifier (e.g., 16) to create a unique code for specific problem types. Enables consistent categorization and analysis of customer complaints across the dealer network.

Standardized description of the customer issue corresponding to the verbatim code. Provides a consistent, professional description of the problem type that complements the customer's own words. Used for reporting, trend analysis, and quality monitoring across the service network.
examples: TRN09 - Oil Leak - Transmission / Transfer Case / PTU
ENG05 - Engine Cranks But Does Not Start

column_desc: Full descriptive name of the verbatim group category. Provides human-readable names for the three-letter group codes, such as ENGINE for ENG, TRANSMISSION for TRN, CLUTCH for CLU, CLIMATE CONTROL for HVA, BODY for BOD, COOLING for CLN, and GENERAL for GEN. Used for reporting labels and user-friendly displays.

Legal registered name of the dealer or service center as per business registration documents. This is the primary identifier for the dealership entity and is used in all official communications, contracts, and business documentation.
examples: J S GROVER AUTOS PVT LTD

column_desc: Service-specific name or alias of the dealer, if different from the legal registered name. Used primarily within service operations for identification purposes. Contains NULL values when the service name is identical to the legal dealer name.

Email address of the sales contact person at the dealer location. Used for sending sales-related communications, quotations, and order confirmations. May be NULL if the sales contact's email is not recorded in the system.
Mobile phone number of the sales contact person at the dealer location. Used for urgent sales communications, customer follow-ups, and coordination. May be NULL if not provided or recorded.

Name of the sales contact person responsible for sales activities at the dealer location. Used for direct communication, lead assignment, and sales performance tracking. May be NULL if not assigned or recorded.

Name of the zonal head responsible for service operations across dealers in a specific geographic zone. This represents the senior management oversight for service quality, performance, and compliance at the regional level.

Name of the Vice President responsible for service operations across the organization. Represents top-level executive oversight for service delivery, customer satisfaction, and service business performance.
Name of the Vice President responsible for sales operations across the organization. Represents top-level executive oversight for sales performance, dealer network growth, and revenue targets.

Indicator flag showing whether the dealer is currently active in the network. 'Y' indicates an active dealer that can conduct business, while 'N' would indicate an inactive or terminated dealer. Used for filtering active dealers in operational reports.

Sales organization identifier that defines the hierarchical structure for sales operations. Used to group dealers under specific sales organizational units for reporting, target setting, and performance management.

Product division code that indicates which product line the dealer is authorized to sell and service. Common values include 'TH' (Thar), 'ZS' (XUV), 'BL' (Bolero), 'IS' (Scorpio), representing different vehicle brands or product categories within the organization.

joins.description: Use this join to connect dealer product divisions with vehicle models to analyze which models are sold/serviced by which dealers and identify product specialization patterns across the dealer network.

column_desc: Name of the parent company or corporate entity that owns or manages the dealer location. Used for corporate-level reporting, financial consolidation, and understanding ownership structures across the dealer network.
Unique code assigned to the parent company for identification in systems and reports. Used as a shorthand reference to the corporate entity in transactional data and reporting.

Code representing the city where the dealer is located. Used for geographic segmentation, regional analysis, and location-based reporting. Links to city master data for city names and additional geographic attributes.

Flag indicating whether the dealer record was selected or included in a batch processing operation. Used in data migration, bulk updates, or ETL processes to track which records have been processed in specific batches.
Date and time when the dealer record was selected or included in a batch processing operation. Used for audit purposes to track when records were processed and to manage data migration timelines.
Area office code or name that represents the regional administrative hub overseeing multiple dealers in a geographic area. Used for hierarchical reporting and regional performance analysis.

Active indicator for the dealer record itself. 'Y' indicates the record is currently active and valid for use in operations and reporting. Used for soft-delete functionality and filtering active records in queries.
Access location indicator that specifies whether the dealer has special access privileges or is designated as an accessible location for specific operations. May be NULL if not applicable.
Geographic longitude coordinate of the dealer location. Used for mapping, route optimization, location-based services, and geographic analysis of dealer coverage. Stored in decimal degrees format.
Geographic latitude coordinate of the dealer location. Used for mapping, route optimization, location-based services, and geographic analysis of dealer coverage. Stored in decimal degrees format.
Mobile phone number of the dealer principal or primary contact at the dealership. Used for high-priority communications, escalations, and strategic business discussions with dealer ownership.
Landline phone number of the dealer principal or primary contact at the dealership. Used as an alternative contact method for business communications.
STD or area code for the landline phone number of the dealer principal. Used in combination with dp_land_no to form complete contact numbers for different geographic regions.
Email address of the dealer principal or primary contact at the dealership. Used for official correspondence, business communications, and strategic communications with dealer ownership.

Name of the Customer Contact Manager (CCM) responsible for managing customer relationships and service experience at the regional level. Acts as the primary point of contact for customer escalations and satisfaction monitoring.

Name of the Area Sales Manager (ASM) responsible for sales performance and dealer management in a specific geographic area. Handles sales targets, dealer relationships, and market development activities.
Generic email address for the dealer's service department. Used for service appointment scheduling, service-related communications, and customer follow-ups regarding vehicle service.
Primary contact number for the dealer's service department. Used by customers to schedule service appointments, inquire about service status, or report service-related issues.
Generic email address for the dealer's sales department. Used for sales inquiries, test drive requests, quotations, and vehicle purchase communications with potential customers.
Primary contact number for the dealer's sales department. Used by customers for sales inquiries, vehicle information requests, test drive scheduling, and purchase negotiations.
Email address or contact information for escalated customer complaints and critical feedback. Used when standard resolution channels are exhausted and issues need attention from higher management.
Flag indicating whether the dealer supports online booking for service appointments. 'Y' indicates customers can book service appointments through digital channels, 'N' indicates manual booking only.

Display indicator that controls whether the dealer information should be shown on public-facing platforms like websites, dealer locators, or customer portals. Used for visibility management in customer-facing applications.
City name as it should be displayed on public-facing platforms. May differ from the actual city name for branding or customer communication purposes.
Secondary distribution channel classification for the dealer. Used for more granular channel analysis and reporting beyond the primary channel classification.
Primary distribution channel classification for the dealer. Defines how the dealer fits into the overall distribution network, such as exclusive dealer, multi-brand outlet, rural distributor, etc.
Type of repair order typically handled by this dealer, such as standard repair, express service, body shop, or quick service. Defines the service capabilities and specialization of the dealer.
Customer Service Point designation or code. Identifies the dealer as an authorized customer service location within the service network hierarchy.
Special Service Point designation or code. Identifies dealers with specialized service capabilities for specific vehicle types, complex repairs, or advanced diagnostics.
Location category that classifies the dealer based on its geographic setting, such as urban, semi-urban, rural, highway, or industrial area. Used for market analysis and service coverage planning.
Alternate dealer location code used in legacy systems or for cross-reference purposes. Helps maintain data consistency when integrating with other systems or during system migrations.

Name of the Customer Contact Manager (CCM) at the corporate or head office level. Responsible for overall customer experience strategy, service quality standards, and escalation management across the dealer network.
Flag indicating whether the dealer record has been updated recently or requires attention in data synchronization processes. Used in data quality management and ETL operations.
Name of the Regional Sales Manager (RSM) responsible for sales performance across multiple dealers in a region. Handles regional sales strategy, dealer support, and sales target achievement.

joins.description: Use this join to connect dealer-level RSM assignments with repair orders to analyze service performance under specific regional sales management. Helps understand the relationship between sales leadership and service outcomes.

column_desc: Name of the Regional Customer Contact Manager (RCCM) responsible for customer experience and service quality across multiple dealers in a region. Handles regional service standards and customer satisfaction initiatives.

joins.description: Use this join to connect dealer-level RCCM assignments with repair orders to analyze customer satisfaction and service quality metrics under specific regional customer contact management.

column_desc: Date and time when the dealer record was last updated in the system. Used for audit purposes, data freshness tracking, and identifying recent changes to dealer information.

City code used for pricing determination. Different cities may have different price lists based on local taxes, freight costs, or market conditions. Links to pricing master data for location-specific pricing.
Service zone designation for the dealer within the East Africa region or similar geographic grouping. Used for regional service operations management and reporting in specific geographic territories.
Sales zone designation for the dealer within the East Africa region or similar geographic grouping. Used for regional sales operations management and reporting in specific geographic territories.
Payer code that identifies the entity responsible for payments to the dealer. Used in financial systems for payment processing, commission calculations, and accounts payable management.
Email address specifically for the dealer's service department communications. May be different from the generic service email for more targeted service operations management.
Mobile phone number specifically for the dealer's service department. Used for urgent service communications, customer updates, and service coordination.
Date when the dealer record was last modified. Used for audit trails, data freshness analysis, and tracking record maintenance activities.
User ID or name of the person who last modified the dealer record. Used for audit purposes to track who made changes to dealer information.
Date when the dealer record was originally created in the system. Used for lifecycle analysis, reporting on dealer network growth, and understanding the age of dealer relationships.
User ID or name of the person who originally created the dealer record. Used for audit purposes to track record creation and onboarding activities.
Name of the Zonal Head responsible for overall operations, including both sales and service, across a specific geographic zone. Represents senior management oversight at the zone level.
Geographic zone name where the dealer is located, such as North Zone, South Zone, East Zone, West Zone. Used for regional segmentation, reporting, and performance analysis across geographic regions.

joins.description: Use this join to analyze service performance and repair order patterns across different geographic zones, enabling zone-wise comparison of service metrics and customer satisfaction.

column_desc: Unloading point or designated area where vehicles are delivered or received at the dealer location. Used in logistics planning for vehicle deliveries and parts shipments.
State code where the dealer is located. Used for tax calculations, regulatory reporting, and geographic analysis at the state level.
Indicator flag showing whether this location offers service operations. 'Y' indicates the dealer provides vehicle service and maintenance, 'N' indicates sales-only location.
Name of the service engineer or technical lead responsible for service operations at the dealer. Acts as the primary technical authority for complex repairs and service quality.
Indicator flag showing whether this location serves as a spare parts distribution point. 'Y' indicates the dealer stocks and distributes spare parts to other service locations.
Indicator flag showing whether this location offers sales operations. 'Y' indicates the dealer sells vehicles, 'N' indicates service-only location.
Name of the sales officer responsible for sales activities at the dealer location. Handles customer inquiries, test drives, and vehicle sales processes.
Regional Sales Manager responsible for overseeing sales operations across multiple dealers in a region. Provides strategic direction, sales support, and performance management.
Region code that identifies the geographic region where the dealer is located. Used for hierarchical reporting and regional aggregation of dealer performance.
Regional Customer Contact Manager responsible for customer experience and service quality across multiple dealers in a region. Handles customer satisfaction initiatives and service standards.
Parent group or corporate entity that owns multiple dealerships. Used for consolidated reporting and understanding dealer network ownership structures.
Location code used for ordering spare parts. May be different from the physical dealer location if parts are ordered through a central or regional warehouse.
Location code used for ordering vehicles for sales inventory. May be different from the physical dealer location if vehicles are ordered through a central or regional distribution center.
Postal code (PIN code) of the dealer location. Used for geographic analysis, logistics planning, and location-based services.
Name of the location or city where the dealer is situated. Used for geographic identification and customer-facing location references.

Unique location code assigned to the dealer for system identification. Serves as the primary reference for the dealer in transactional systems, including repair orders, parts orders, and sales transactions.

joins.description: Use this join to connect dealer master information with repair order transactions. Essential for analyzing service performance, revenue, and customer satisfaction metrics by dealer location.

column_desc: Physical street address of the dealer location. Used for customer visits, deliveries, and geographic identification.
Locality or neighborhood name where the dealer is situated. Provides more granular location information within a city for easier customer navigation.
Distribution channel classification for the dealer. Defines the primary channel through which the dealer operates, such as retail, fleet, government, or rural channel.
Date when the dealer record was deleted or marked for deletion. Used for soft-delete functionality and tracking dealer deactivation or removal from the network.
Classification of dealer type, such as exclusive dealer, multi-brand outlet, service center only, or sales-only outlet. Defines the business model and operational scope of the dealer.
Tax Identification Number (TIN) of the dealer for tax reporting and compliance. Used in financial transactions and statutory reporting.
Tax Deduction and Collection Account Number (TAN) of the dealer for tax deduction at source purposes. Used for income tax compliance.
Central Sales Tax (CST) number of the dealer for interstate sales transactions. Used for tax compliance on cross-state sales.

Dealer code that uniquely identifies the dealer in the system. This is the primary key for the dealer dimension table and is used as the main identifier in all dealer-related operations, transactions, and reports.
Row number or sequence identifier for the record, often used in data processing, pagination, or as a system-generated identifier for internal tracking.
Name of the area where the dealer is located. Provides geographic context at the area level for regional analysis and reporting.
Name of the zone where the dealer is located. Provides high-level geographic grouping for strategic analysis and executive reporting.
Name of the Area Sales Manager (ASM) responsible for this dealer. Handles day-to-day sales operations, dealer support, and sales performance at the area level.

joins.description: Use this join to connect area sales managers with repair orders to analyze service performance under specific area sales management and identify correlations between sales leadership and service outcomes.

column_desc: Name of the Customer Contact Manager (CCM) responsible for customer experience at this dealer. Handles customer satisfaction, complaint resolution, and service quality monitoring.

joins.description: Use this join to connect customer contact managers with repair orders to analyze customer satisfaction metrics, complaint resolution times, and service quality indicators under specific CCM supervision.

column_desc: Name of the Regional Sales Manager (RSM) responsible for this dealer. Oversees sales performance across multiple dealers in a region and provides strategic sales direction.

Name of the Regional Customer Contact Manager (RCCM) responsible for customer experience across multiple dealers in a region. Sets service standards and handles regional customer satisfaction initiatives.
Name of the Zonal Head responsible for sales operations across the zone. Provides strategic sales leadership at the zonal level.
Name of the Zonal Head responsible for customer contact and service operations across the zone. Provides strategic service leadership at the zonal level.
Date when the record was created in the Master Data Platform (MDP). Used for data lineage tracking and understanding when dealer information was loaded into the data warehouse.
Job identifier for the ETL process that loaded this record into the Master Data Platform. Used for data lineage, troubleshooting, and understanding data loading history.
Business date associated with the data load into the Master Data Platform. Used for time-based partitioning and understanding the business effective date of the data.

Date when the repair order was cancelled. Populated only when the repair order status is 'CAN' (Cancelled). Used for tracking cancellation patterns, analyzing reasons for cancellations, and measuring service booking efficiency.
Flag indicating whether Spot Care services were applied to the repair order. Spot Care typically refers to express or quick service offerings. Values include 'Y' (Yes), 'N' (No), or NULL.

Indicator showing whether the vehicle was towed to the service center. 'Y' indicates the vehicle arrived via towing service, typically due to breakdown or accident. Used for analyzing roadside assistance cases and emergency service patterns.

Vehicle Identification Number - a unique 17-character code assigned to every motor vehicle when manufactured. Serves as the vehicle's fingerprint and is used to track vehicle history, specifications, and service records throughout its lifetime.

joins.description: Use this join to decode vehicle specifications from the VIN and link to model master data. The first 8 characters typically identify the model and variant.

column_desc: Date when the repair order was created in the system. Marks the beginning of the service transaction and is used for aging calculations, service volume analysis, and tracking service patterns over time.
Timestamp of repair order creation, including both date and time. Provides precise timing for service appointment analysis, workload planning, and understanding service patterns throughout the day.
Date when the invoice was generated for the repair order. Populated only when the repair order status is 'BIL' (Billed). Used for revenue recognition, billing cycle analysis, and payment tracking.
Timestamp of invoice generation, including both date and time. Provides precise timing for billing analysis, payment processing, and understanding service completion patterns.
Date when the repair order was closed after all work was completed and the vehicle was delivered to the customer. Marks the completion of the service lifecycle and is used for cycle time analysis.

Current status of the repair order. Common values include 'CAN' (Cancelled), 'BIL' (Billed), and other statuses indicating the stage of the service process. Used for operational tracking and reporting.
Odometer reading at the time of vehicle drop-off for service. Measured in kilometers and used for service interval tracking, warranty validation, and vehicle usage analysis.
Surrogate key for the service appointment header. Links the repair order to the original service appointment booking for analyzing appointment-to-service conversion and scheduling efficiency.
Date when the repair order was billed. Similar to BILL_DATE but may represent a different business process step. Used for billing cycle analysis.
Date when the repair order was cancelled. Duplicate of RO_CANCL_DATE, maintained for compatibility or historical reasons.

Surrogate key uniquely identifying the repair order header record in the data warehouse. This system-generated identifier serves as the primary key for the repair order header table and enables efficient joining with all related fact tables.

joins.description: Use this join to analyze customer-reported issues and service requests associated with each repair order.
Mandatory join to access detailed labor operations performed during the service visit.
Mandatory join to access parts consumed during the service visit.

column_desc: Parent group or corporate entity that owns the dealer location. Links to dealer dimension for understanding ownership structures and consolidated reporting across dealer groups.

joins.description: Use this join to analyze service performance across different dealer ownership groups and identify group-level trends.

column_desc: Location code of the dealer or service center where the repair was performed. Links to dealer dimension for location-specific analysis and reporting.

joins.description: Mandatory join to enrich repair orders with dealer location information including zone, region, and contact details for geographic analysis.

column_desc: Repair Order number that uniquely identifies the service transaction in the operational system. This alphanumeric identifier is used for customer communications, service records, and cross-referencing with other service-related documents.

joins.description: Use this join to connect repair orders with customer-reported issues using the business key.

column_desc: Total amount charged for parts including taxes and after discounts. Represents the final amount customer pays for parts. Used for revenue analysis and parts profitability tracking.

Total amount charged for labor including taxes and after discounts. Represents the final amount customer pays for labor services. Used for service revenue analysis and technician productivity tracking.
Document amount or total invoice amount including parts, labor, and any other charges. Represents the final bill amount for the service transaction.
Base amount for parts before taxes and discounts. Used for calculating taxes, discounts, and understanding parts revenue components.
Base amount for labor before taxes and discounts. Used for calculating taxes, discounts, and understanding labor revenue components.

Name of the Service Advisor who handled the repair order. The Service Advisor is the primary customer contact for the service visit, responsible for understanding customer concerns, recommending services, and communicating with customers.
Date and time when the service advisor promised the vehicle would be ready for delivery. Used for tracking delivery commitment accuracy and customer service level measurement.
Revised promised delivery date when the original commitment could not be met. Captures changes in delivery estimates due to parts availability, additional work, or other delays.
Revisit indicator showing whether this repair order represents a return visit for the same issue. 'Y' indicates the customer is returning for additional work related to a previous service.
Model code identifying the vehicle model being serviced. Links to model master for detailed vehicle specifications.

joins.description: Use this join to enrich repair orders with detailed vehicle specifications for model-specific service analysis.

column_desc: Surrogate key for the repair order billing header. Links to billing details for invoice-level analysis.
Flag indicating whether online payment was used for the service bill. 'Y' indicates customer paid online, 'N' indicates other payment methods.
Market segment code classifying the vehicle's intended use. Values include 'PERSONAL' for private vehicles, 'COMMERCIAL' for business vehicles, and 'LMM' (Light Motor Vehicle) categories.

joins.description: Use this join to analyze service patterns across different vehicle segments.

column_desc: Express service indicator showing whether the vehicle received express/quick service. 'N' indicates standard service, other values may indicate express service types.
Chassis number of the vehicle. Often the last portion of the VIN used as a shorter identifier for the vehicle in service records and communications.
Key account segment classification for corporate or fleet customers. Used for specialized handling and reporting of important accounts.
Indicator showing whether the vehicle is government-owned. Used for specialized billing, reporting, and compliance requirements for government vehicles.
Color code of the vehicle. Links to color master for color descriptions and analysis of color-specific trends.

joins.description: Use this join to analyze service patterns by vehicle color.

column_desc: Original Equipment Manufacturer model code. Links to model master for vehicle specifications and parts compatibility.

joins.description: Use this join to connect repair orders with OEM model specifications for parts and service compatibility.

column_desc: Product division code indicating the vehicle brand or product line. Examples include BL (Bolero), RV (REVA), 5 (Scorpio), BS (Bolero), IS (Scorpio), TH (Thar).

joins.description: Use this join to analyze service performance by product division.
Use this join to analyze dealer service capabilities by product division.

column_desc: Parts charge amount, often representing a specific component or summary of parts charges. May differ from PARTS_TOTL_AMNT in business context.
Labor charge amount, often representing a specific component or summary of labor charges. May differ from LABR_TOTL_AMNT in business context.
Same Day Delivery flag indicating whether the service was completed and vehicle delivered on the same day. Used for measuring service efficiency and customer satisfaction.
Flag indicating if the repair order took greater than 72 hours to complete. Used for cycle time analysis and identifying extended service durations.
Flag indicating if the repair order took less than or equal to 72 hours to complete. Used for cycle time analysis and measuring service efficiency.
Flag indicating if the repair order took less than or equal to 48 hours to complete. Used for measuring express service performance.
Flag indicating if the repair order took less than or equal to 24 hours to complete. Used for measuring same-day service performance.
Difference in days between promised delivery date and actual closure date. Used for measuring delivery commitment accuracy and customer satisfaction.
Technical Evaluation Authorization flag. Indicates whether technical evaluation was authorized for the repair order.
Customer Evaluation Authorization flag. Indicates whether customer evaluation was authorized for the repair order.
M-Traq flag indicating whether the vehicle is equipped with M-Traq telematics system. Used for connected vehicle services and remote diagnostics analysis.

Vehicle classification code based on European/Indian motor vehicle classification. M1 indicates passenger vehicles, M2/M3 indicate buses, etc. Used for regulatory compliance and vehicle categorization.
Composite key combining location, dealer, and segment information. Used for fast lookups and data partitioning.
Composite key combining chassis number and repair order ID. Used for unique identification of repair orders in reporting and analysis.
Composite key for employee-related analysis. Combines location, dealer, and employee identifier for tracking service advisor performance.
Composite key for service bay analysis. Combines location, dealer, and bay information for tracking bay utilization and technician productivity.
Day of the week when the repair order was closed. Used for analyzing service completion patterns by day of week.
Original warranty start date for the vehicle. Marks the beginning of warranty coverage period, typically the vehicle purchase or delivery date.
Roadside Assistance registration status. Indicates whether the vehicle is registered for RSA services and the status of that registration.
Maximum number of days from Date of Sale for certain eligibility calculations. Used for warranty and service plan eligibility.
Date used for eligibility calculations. May represent vehicle arrival date or other reference date for determining warranty or service plan coverage.
Flag indicating whether the vehicle is registered for Roadside Assistance. 'RSA Registered' or 'RSA Not Registered' values determine RSA coverage.
Date when Roadside Assistance coverage expires. Used for validating RSA claims and understanding coverage periods.
Flag indicating whether the vehicle is eligible for warranty or service plan coverage. 'Eligible Vehicle' or 'Not Eligible Vehicle' values determine coverage status.
Date when the original vehicle warranty expires. Used for warranty claim validation and service planning.
Kilometer limit at which the original warranty expires. Combined with date to determine warranty coverage based on whichever comes first.
Flag indicating whether the repair order was covered under warranty. 'Warranty' or 'Non Warranty' values determine billing and coverage treatment.
End date for certain validity periods. Often set to a far future date (9999-12-31) for active records or specific dates for time-bound validity.
Flag indicating whether the vehicle has valid Shield (extended warranty) coverage. 'Valid Shield' or 'Invalid Shield' values determine coverage status.
Expiry date for service schemes or extended warranty plans. Used for validating coverage and planning renewals.
Flag indicating whether the vehicle is registered for Shield (extended warranty) coverage. 'Shield Registered' or 'Shield Not Registered' values determine registration status.
Maximum Retail Price for parts or services. Used for pricing analysis and discount calculations.
Net Dealer Price - the price at which parts are supplied to the dealer. Used for margin analysis and profitability calculations.
Total quantity of parts used in the repair order. Used for inventory analysis and parts consumption tracking.
Classification of repair order based on how long it remained open. Used for aging analysis and identifying delays.
Service Advisor feedback statistics. May contain ratings, comments, or metrics related to service advisor performance.
Flag indicating whether accessories were installed or sold during the service visit. Used for accessories sales tracking.
Division key combining location and product division. Used for division-level reporting and analysis.
Vehicle registration number (license plate). Used for vehicle identification and customer communications.
Remarks or comments about the repair order. May include special instructions, notes about vehicle condition, or other relevant information.
Service bay active indicator. 'Y' indicates the bay was active and used for this repair order.
Application type or category for the repair order. May indicate the type of service or business application.
Warranty aging in days at the time of repair order creation. Used for warranty analysis and trend identification.
Warranty aging in days as of today's date. Used for current warranty status analysis.
Description of the service type performed. Examples include 'Charging', 'En-route Repair', 'PRESALE', indicating the nature of the service.
Service category description providing broader categorization of the service type. Examples include 'Charging', 'En-Route', 'Pre-Sale/PDI'.
Composite key combining model and color information. Used for vehicle specification analysis and reporting.

joins.description: Use this join to analyze service patterns by specific model and color combinations.

column_desc: Source of the repair order, such as 'Campaign', 'RSA / Breakdown', 'Walk-In', 'MMT'. Indicates how the service request originated.
Name of the service type. May provide more detailed service classification than SERVC_TYPE_DESC.
Reason for delay when same-day delivery was not achieved. Captures explanations for service delays.
examples: Delay due to Major Job not completed in workshop working hours

column_desc: Reason for variation between estimated and actual service costs. Used for understanding estimation accuracy and customer communications.
examples: Customer requested additional job within 1 hr window before RO closing

column_desc: Reason for cancellation when repair order status is 'CAN'. Used for analyzing cancellation patterns and improving service processes.
examples: RO open with incorrect Service type

column_desc: Reason for delay in completing the repair order. Used for identifying bottlenecks and improving service efficiency.
examples: Customer requested additional job within 1 hr window before RO closing

column_desc: Reason why the repair order was pending or delayed. Used for analyzing work-in-progress and service bottlenecks.
Type of pickup service used for the vehicle. 'Customer refused' indicates customer declined pickup service, other values may indicate different pickup arrangements.
Type of drop-off or delivery service for the vehicle. 'Customer refused' indicates customer declined delivery service, other values may indicate different delivery arrangements.
Type of document generated for the repair order, such as 'Cash' or 'Credit Card'. Indicates the billing document type.
Type of bill issued, such as 'Cash' or 'Credit Card'. Indicates the payment method or billing arrangement.
Type of service campaign if the repair was part of a campaign. 'Within Workshop' indicates work done within the workshop as part of a campaign.
Name of the technician who performed the service. Used for tracking technician productivity and skill analysis.
Name of the product division corresponding to PRODCT_DIVSN code. Examples include 'Bolero', 'REVA', 'Pick-up', 'Scorpio', 'THAR'.

joins.description: Use this join to connect repair orders with model groups for product-line analysis.

column_desc: Indicator showing whether the vehicle is used as a taxi. 'TAXI' indicates commercial taxi use, 'NON TAXI' indicates private use.
Type of service bay used for the repair. Examples include 'Service Bay', 'MMS/DSS/WYH/URT/2WR' indicating different bay types.
Flag indicating whether this is a revisit for the same issue. 'Y' indicates customer returning for additional work related to previous service.
Flag indicating whether this is a repeat repair for the same issue within a certain timeframe. Used for quality analysis and identifying recurring problems.
Group classification for the service performed. 'Others' indicates services not falling into standard categories, 'Pre-Sale/PDI' indicates pre-delivery inspection services.

Surrogate key linking the parts line item to the parent repair order header. This system-generated identifier enables joining parts consumption data with repair order details, labor operations, and customer verbatim for comprehensive service analysis.

joins.description: Mandatory join to associate parts line items with the parent repair order for header-level context including vehicle details, service dates, and customer information.
Use this join to analyze the relationship between customer-reported issues and the specific parts replaced during service.
Use this join to correlate parts replaced with labor operations performed for comprehensive service analysis.

column_desc: Surrogate key for the repair order billing header. Links parts line items to the billing record for invoice-level analysis and financial reporting.

Surrogate key uniquely identifying the parts line item in the data warehouse. Serves as the primary key for the parts detail table and enables precise tracking of individual parts transactions within repair orders.
Unique alphanumeric code identifying the specific part in the inventory system. Used for parts lookup, inventory management, and cross-referencing with parts master data.
Descriptive name of the part, including specifications, dimensions, and application. Provides detailed identification of the part for service technicians, inventory staff, and parts ordering.
examples: CED PANEL ASSY HEADER LWR FRT
CED PANEL BODY SIDE OUTER RH
CED PANEL BODY SIDE OUTER LH
CED BRKT ASSY FRONT FENDER RH
CED BRKT ASSY FRONT FENDER LH

column_desc: Number of units of the specific part used in the repair order. Essential for inventory management, parts consumption analysis, and calculating total parts costs.
Unit price of the part before discounts and taxes. Used as the base for calculating total parts cost and analyzing parts pricing.
Total amount for the part line item before taxes (quantity × rate). Represents the base cost of parts before additional charges.
Tax amount applied to the parts line item. Includes GST or other applicable taxes based on local tax regulations and part classification.
Discount amount applied to the parts line item. Zero indicates no discount was applied to this part.
Total amount for the parts line item including taxes and after discounts (partAmount + part_tax - part_discnt_amnt). Represents the final amount charged to the customer for this part.
Original Equipment Manufacturer part indicator. 'Y' indicates the part is an OEM genuine part, ensuring quality and warranty compliance.

ABC classification code for inventory management. Parts are categorized as A (high value, low volume), B (medium value and volume), or C (low value, high volume) for inventory optimization and control.
High-Medium-Low value classification for parts. 'H' indicates high-value parts, 'M' indicates medium-value parts, 'L' indicates low-value parts. Used for inventory valuation and risk analysis.
Total cost of the part including purchase price, freight, insurance, and other charges until delivery to warehouse. Used for profitability analysis and inventory valuation.
RIS (Returnable/Issuable) indicator for parts management. 'R' indicates returnable parts, 'I' indicates issuable parts, 'S' may indicate special handling requirements.
Purchase price paid by the dealer to acquire the part. Used for margin analysis and cost control.
Selling price of the part to the customer before taxes and discounts. May be different from MRP based on dealer pricing policies.
Maximum Retail Price - the highest price at which the part can be sold to customers as per regulations. Used for pricing compliance and discount calculations.
Harmonized System of Nomenclature code for customs and tax classification. Used for GST calculation and regulatory compliance.
Net dealer price after all discounts and adjustments. Represents the actual cost to the dealer after considering all pricing factors.
Discount rate applied to the part. Zero indicates no discount was applied to this part.
Gross Net Dealer Price - intermediate pricing metric used in dealer margin calculations.
Net Dealer Price - final price at which the part is supplied to the dealer. Used for dealer margin analysis and profitability calculations.
Percentage share of this part in the total bill. 0 indicates the full amount is allocated to this billing entity.
Total bill amount for the repair order. Used for understanding the contribution of this part to the overall service invoice.
Description of the part category. 'Spares' indicates the part is a service spare part used for repairs and maintenance.
Type of billing arrangement for the part. 'Insurance' indicates the part cost is covered by insurance claim rather than customer direct payment.
Type of discount applied to the part. '%' indicates percentage-based discount, other values may indicate fixed amount or other discount types.

Name of the product division to which the part belongs. Examples include 'UV & Common Spares' for utility vehicles, 'SCORPIO-N SPARES' for Scorpio model parts, indicating the vehicle line compatibility.

joins.description: Use this join to connect parts with vehicle models for analyzing part usage patterns by model.
Use this join to analyze parts usage by dealer product division authorization.

column_desc: Type classification of the part. 'GENERAL' indicates standard service parts, other values may indicate specialized part types.
Group classification for parts categorization. 'Spares' indicates the part belongs to the spares category for inventory and reporting purposes.
Common part category for standardized reporting. 'Parts' indicates this is a standard part category across the organization.
Additional part category classification. 'RR Parts' may indicate parts related to repair and replacement operations.

Surrogate key that uniquely identifies the repair order header in the MH_RO_HDR_DETAILS table. This column serves as the foreign key link between labor line items and their parent repair order header. Used to group all labor charges associated with a specific service visit or repair order.

joins.description: Use this join to retrieve the complete repair order header details (vehicle info, dates, odometer, etc.) for each labor line item. Essential for analyzing labor performance by vehicle, dealer, or time period.

column_desc: Surrogate key linking to the billing header for the repair order. Used to associate labor line items with their corresponding billing or invoice header information. Helps track which labor items were included in which billing transaction or invoice.

joins.description: Use this join when analyzing billing and invoicing details for labor services, such as invoice dates, payment terms, or total billed amounts per labor line item.

column_desc: Unique surrogate key for each labor line item in the repair order billing details. This is the primary key for the MH_RO_LABOUR table, uniquely identifying each individual labor service record. Used to distinguish between different labor operations performed during a single service visit.

Standard or book time allocated for the labor operation based on manufacturer specifications. Represents the expected time (in hours) to complete the service under normal conditions. Used to compare actual vs. standard efficiency, calculate labor productivity, and validate labor charges.

Surrogate key linking to the labor master table that contains predefined labor operations, their standard descriptions, and default rates. Used to reference standardized labor codes and their associated metadata across different repair orders.

joins.description: Use this join to retrieve standardized labor operation details such as default descriptions, standard labor codes, and manufacturer-recommended labor times. Helpful for comparing actual charges against standard rates or analyzing which labor operations are most frequently performed.

column_desc: Description of the labor service performed. Provides a human-readable explanation of the work completed, such as 'WHEEL BALANCING' or 'PAINTING CHARGES'. Used in reports and invoices to communicate the nature of the service to customers and service advisors.

Tax amount applied to the labor service. Represents the calculated tax (e.g., GST, VAT, sales tax) based on the applicable tax rate for the labor operation. Used in financial reporting to track tax liability and ensure accurate invoice totals.

Base amount for the labor service before tax and discounts. Calculated as labor rate multiplied by charged hours. Represents the pre-tax value of the labor performed, used as the foundation for calculating taxes and determining total labor charges.

Hourly labor rate charged for the service. Represents the cost per hour for the specific labor operation, which may vary based on labor type, technician skill level, or dealer pricing policies. Used to calculate labor charges and analyze pricing strategies.

Actual hours charged for the labor service. May differ from standard hours based on actual time taken, special circumstances, or negotiated adjustments. Used to calculate the final labor amount and track efficiency metrics between actual and standard hours.

Discount amount applied to the labor service. Represents any reductions from the standard labor charge, such as promotional discounts, goodwill adjustments, or customer loyalty programs. Used to track discount patterns and analyze revenue impact.

Total amount for the labor service including all charges, taxes, and after discounts. The final amount that appears on the customer's invoice for this specific labor line item. Calculated as labrAmount + labr_tax - labr_discnt_amnt. Used for financial reconciliation and revenue reporting.

Total bill amount for the entire repair order. While appearing on each labor line item, this represents the aggregate total of the parent repair order including all labor, parts, and other charges. Used for consistency checking and understanding the proportion of labor costs to total bill.

Classification of how the labor is billed to the customer. Common values include 'Customer Paid' (customer bears the cost) or 'Dealer FOC' (dealer provides service free of charge as goodwill or warranty). Used to analyze revenue streams, warranty costs, and dealer goodwill adjustments.

High-level categorization of the labor type, typically indicating the source or standard definition. Values like 'OEM Defined' indicate that the labor operation follows manufacturer specifications. Used to distinguish between standard manufacturer operations and custom dealer-defined services.

Group or category code for the labor operation, typically representing the department or type of service (e.g., 'MAT-779' for materials/accessories, 'WHT-J06' for wheel/tire services). Used for departmental analysis and grouping similar types of labor operations.

Standardized code or identifier for the labor operation, often from the manufacturer's labor operation catalog (e.g., 'ACCSFIT' for accessories fitment, '1640F' for wheel balancing). Used for consistent reporting across different dealers and for analyzing frequency of specific operations.
